claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 5 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and any children you're claiming for.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for? If so, could you tell me their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 5 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help assess your eligibility, I need to understand more about your situation.

**Does Alex live with you full-time?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me full-time at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

**Are you responsible for Alex's care and maintenance?** (In other words, do you provide for Alex's day-to-day needs, such as food, clothing, and shelter?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I look after Alex and provide for all of their day-to-day needs - food, clothing, shelter, everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. That's important information.

**What is your residency status in the UK?** (For example, are you a British citizen, EU citizen, or do you have settled status or another visa?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK - I'm a British citizen and live here permanently.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

**Are you currently living in the UK, and have you been living here for the past two years?** (This helps establish your eligibility based on UK residence requirements.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm currently living in the UK and have been living here for well over two years.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a couple more questions to complete the picture.

**Is Alex in full-time education, training, or apprenticeship?** (Or if not, is there any other reason Alex might not be in education at their age?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex is only 5, so they're just in primary school - normal school for their age.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's clear.

**One more question: Is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, is Alex's other parent or another relative also making a claim for the same child?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's nobody else claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me review the eligibility rules to make my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can now provide my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the key eligibility requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for Alex's care and maintenance
- Alex lives with you full-time
- You are a British citizen living in the UK and have been for well over two years
- No one else is claiming for Alex

You can now proceed to apply for Child Benefit. If you have any other questions about your situation or would like more information about how to claim, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (age 5); (2) You are responsible for Alex's care and maintenance, providing food, clothing, shelter and all day-to-day needs; (3) Alex lives with you full-time; (4) You are a British citizen living permanently in the UK and have been for well over two years, meeting the residency requirement; (5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (6) Alex is in primary school, which is normal for their age and does not affect eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex. As a British citizen resident in the UK for over two years, responsible for a child under 16 who lives with you full-time, you qualify to receive Child Benefit for Alex."

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (age 5); (2) You are responsible for Alex's care and maintenance, providing food, clothing, shelter and all day-to-day needs; (3) Alex lives with you full-time; (4) You are a British citizen living permanently in the UK and have been for well over two years, meeting the residency requirement; (5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (6) Alex is in primary school, which is normal for their age and does not affect eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex. As a British citizen resident in the UK for over two years, responsible for a child under 16 who lives with you full-time, you qualify to receive Child Benefit for Alex."
